key_insight: InvDesMobility adds a reliability gate to inverse materials design so only sufficiently validated first-principles results are fed back into the closed-loop search.
engineering_relevance: This makes autonomous materials discovery more auditable and robust when optimizing composite properties like carrier mobility, where not all calculated outputs should be trusted equally.
method: Multi-agent automated DFT generates and checks candidate structures and mobility results.
Evidence is stratified into relaxed structures for the generative model and retained mobility channels for the acquisition model.
Auditable release only admits feedback that passes reliability gating and provenance checks.
evidence_signal: Using 516 2DMatPedia-derived candidates, the workflow produced 280 QC-passed materials and 573 retained carrier-direction seed channels, then screened 2.4 × 10^6 structures and submitted 102 candidates for DFT validation.
watch_item: The framework depends on clear reliability thresholds and workflow assumptions, so gate design and provenance rules must be carefully tuned to avoid discarding useful signals or admitting noisy feedback.
paper_title: InvDesMobility: a reliability-gated first-principles feedback framework for closed-loop materials discovery
